title: oleosanginae
intro: The oleosanginae rank is a subtribe, which is a taxonomic rank below the tribe and above the genus within the plant classification system. It represents a grouping of genera that share similar characteristics and are more closely related to each other than to other genera within the same tribe. Subtribes like oleosanginae help to further refine the classification of plants, allowing for more precise identification and understanding of their evolutionary relationships. Examples of subtribes include oleosanginae, associated with orders like the Asparagales, Malpighiales, and Poales.

orders.order_desc: Gleicheniales is a group of plants within the class Polypodiopsida of the division Pteridophyta in the kingdom Plantae.
